name: KSHV_oriLyt_origins
description: KSHV lytic origins of replication: two near-identical oriLyt elements (oriLyt-L at ~24 kb, oriLyt-R at ~120 kb), each ~1.2 kb containing AT-rich palindromic core, GC-rich repeats, and binding sites for the lytic transactivator RTA/ORF50, K-bZIP/ORF-K8, and the viral pre-replication complex (ORF6/8/9/40/41/44/56/59). The two oriLyt loci are highly homologous (~95% identity) — paralog-disambiguation required when assigning replication-fork or breakpoint reads.
